name: Gāyatrī Mantra
devanagari: ॐ भूर्भुवः स्वः । तत्सवितुर्वरेण्यं । भर्गो देवस्य धीमहि । धियो यो नः प्रचोदयात् ॥
iast: oṃ bhūr bhuvaḥ svaḥ | tat savitur vareṇyaṃ | bhargo devasya dhīmahi | dhiyo yo naḥ pracodayāt ||
transliteration_simple: Om Bhur Bhuvah Svah | Tat Savitur Varenyam | Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi | Dhiyo Yo Nah Prachodayat ||
english_translation: We meditate on the divine light of the Sun (Savitur). May it illuminate our intellect and guide us toward righteous action.
meaning_summary: A prayer for illumination of the intellect (buddhi). The most revered Vedic mantra from the Ṛgveda (3.62.10), attributed to Viśvāmitra.
phoneme_sequence: Long nasalized oṃ - the primordial sound
Aspirated dh + LONG ī - critical vowel length
Long ā + final dental t - clean release
sanskrit_specific_guidance: Hold long vowels (ī in dhīmahi, ā in pracodayāt) approximately 1.8-2x longer than short vowels.
Visarga (ḥ) at end of bhuvaḥ, svaḥ, naḥ should be a gentle outward breath, not a hard stop.
The anusvara (ṃ) in vareṇyaṃ and pracodayāt should resonate nasally without closing the mouth fully.
Maintain steady pitch on sustained vowels; avoid scooping up or down unintentionally.
The rhythm follows the Gāyatrī meter: groups of 8 syllables with slight emphasis on 1st and 5th of each pāda.
common_mistakes: Shortening the ī in 'dhīmahi' (most frequent error)
Pronouncing retroflex ṇ as dental n in 'vareṇyaṃ'
Rushing the final 'pracodayāt' - the long ā needs space
Inconsistent volume or pitch drift across the four pādas
